title: [VMIC-A2] TtsRouting config enum + virtual_mic_device field
scope: TtsRouting enum (Speakers|VirtualMic|Both), virtual_mic_device field, backwards-compatible config migration, validation errors for invalid route/device combos

test_cases.config_legacy_tts_output_device_loads: pass — route defaults to Speakers; tts_output_device preserved; validates OK
test_cases.config_virtual_mic_requires_device: pass — typed validation error mentioning tts_routing and virtual_mic_device
test_cases.config_virtual_mic_device_rejects_malfor: pass — blank, padded, and control-character device names are rejected
test_cases.tts_routing_both_roundtrips: pass — serde uses snake_case "both"; roundtrip equals original

commands.name: acceptance-test-cases
commands.command: cargo +stable-x86_64-pc-windows-gnu test --bin tui-translator -- config_legacy_tts_output_device_loads && cargo +stable-x86_64-pc-windows-gnu test --bin tui-translator -- config_virtual_mic_requires_device && cargo +stable-x86_64-pc-windows-gnu test --bin tui-translator -- config_virtual_mic_device_rejects_malformed_name && cargo +stable-x86_64-pc-windows-gnu test --bin tui-translator -- tts_routing_both_roundtrips

notes: TtsRouting uses #[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")] matching existing config style.
tts_routing is omitted from serialized JSON when it equals the default (Speakers), preserving config file cleanliness.
virtual_mic_device is omitted from serialized JSON when None, matching existing Option<String> field patterns.
validate() returns a typed bail! error mentioning both tts_routing and virtual_mic_device when VirtualMic/Both is used without a device.
virtual_mic_device rejects blank, leading/trailing whitespace, and control-character values even before virtual-mic routing is enabled.
Legacy configs with only tts_output_device deserialize with tts_routing=Speakers via #[serde(default)].
requires_restart() updated to include tts_routing and virtual_mic_device changes.
config.example.json documents tts_routing and virtual_mic_device in the _comment block and exposes safe default example values.
All 151 config-filtered tests continue to pass.
